concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains meaningful images on the first page, specifically the North Carolina State Seal and the NC DEQ logo. The HTML output contains tags, but the image manifest indicates that all extracted images have 'valid: false' due to a PIL module error. This means the base64 data is missing or corrupt, and the images will render as broken links. Furthermore, the agency logos themselves do not appear to have been extracted independently.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 contains the North Carolina state seal at the top and the NC DEQ logo at the bottom left.
concerns.output_quote: The Image Manifest shows 'valid: false' and 'error': 'No module named \'PIL\'' for all images, meaning they will fail to render.

concerns.analysis: The HTML output includes tags whose alt text clearly indicates they are full-page screenshots or large sections of the document (e.g., 'A formal business letter...', 'A formal legal notice...'). These images are placed directly alongside the fully transcribed text of those same pages. Although the images are broken (as noted in V2), the HTML structure demonstrates the pipeline attempted to render the pages as full-page images alongside the extracted text, which constitutes duplication of t
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The text of the letter on Page 1 and the Notice on Page 2.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML includes tags intended to display full-page screenshots (alt='A formal business letter regarding a revised Notice...', alt='A formal legal notice from the North Carolina Department...') immediately before or after the extracted text of the same content.

concerns.description: Scanned document: source has 0 extractable text but output contains 377 words (OCR'd from images)
concerns.analysis: baseline_words=0, gemini_words=377. Entire document is image-based; output likely duplicates content as both page image and extracted text.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 2.5x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=2.50. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
The source document includes underlined text for emphasis in the fourth paragraph on page 2. This underline emphasis is lost in the extraction.
